transcript:
Here's what's happening: horizontal bands of muted light — bone white, faint amber, a bruised violet — drifting slowly upward, like watching radio static settle into weather patterns.

Look at how these bands are built. Each one, in the setup, is called a "sweep" and gets assigned a speed drawn from a list of prime numbers — the comment says "incommensurable frequencies create standing waves." Because those speeds never share a common rhythm, the bands drift past each other without ever quite syncing up or repeating. And notice the piece doesn't start from nothing — before you even see the first frame, there's a loop labeled "warmup" that runs the whole system twelve times in silence. The comment calls it "the system arrives mid-process... we enter a field already flowing." You're never watching this thing begin.

That's the quiet trick here: there's no moment of activation, no first note. The piece insists it was already listening before you arrived, and it'll keep listening after you look away — attention with no on-switch, just continuous readiness.

cues.context: the setup, is called a "sweep" and gets assigned a speed

cues.target: Speed derived from primes — incommensurable frequencies create standing waves
cues.context: numbers — the comment says "incommensurable frequencies create standing waves." Because those speeds never share

cues.target: We enter a field already flowing, channels already carved
cues.context: it "the system arrives mid-process... we enter a field already flowing." You're never watching this thing
